name: Hospital / Medical Device Compromise
description: Attack tree modelling compromise of a hospital network targeting Electronic Medical Records (EMR), infusion pumps, medical imaging, and connected bedside devices
root_objective: Compromise hospital systems to disrupt patient care or steal medical data

nodes.title: Compromise Hospital for Care Disruption or Data Theft
nodes.description: Attack hospital IT and medical device networks to disrupt patient care, steal PHI, or hold systems to ransom

nodes.title: Gain Access to Hospital Network
nodes.description: Establish network-level access to the hospital's clinical or administrative network segments

nodes.title: Phish Clinical Staff
nodes.description: Target nurses, doctors, or admin staff with medical-themed phishing lures

nodes.title: Exploit Exposed RDP/Citrix
nodes.description: Compromise internet-facing remote access used by physicians and telehealth providers

nodes.title: Connect via Guest Wi-Fi Bridge
nodes.description: Exploit flat network between guest Wi-Fi and clinical VLAN due to poor segmentation

nodes.title: Compromise Electronic Medical Records
nodes.description: Access or encrypt EMR/EHR systems containing patient health information

nodes.title: Encrypt EMR Database (Ransomware)
nodes.description: Deploy ransomware targeting the EMR database servers to lock patient records

nodes.title: Exfiltrate Patient Records (PHI)
nodes.description: Steal protected health information for sale on dark markets or blackmail of patients

nodes.title: Attack Connected Medical Devices
nodes.description: Compromise networked medical devices to disrupt patient care

nodes.title: Manipulate Infusion Pump Dosage
nodes.description: Alter drug infusion pump settings via unencrypted network protocols to change dosage rates

nodes.title: Disrupt Medical Imaging (PACS/DICOM)
nodes.description: Attack PACS servers via unencrypted DICOM protocol to delete, modify, or encrypt medical images

nodes.title: Compromise Patient Monitor Network
nodes.description: Access bedside monitors to display false vital signs or suppress critical alarms

nodes.title: Legacy Medical Devices (Unpatchable)
nodes.description: Medical devices running Windows XP/7 or embedded Linux with no vendor patches available, exempt from hospital patching
